item.title: Gaming: Red Dead Redemption 2
item.description:
Game/Publisher/Developer

Red Dead Redemption 2, Rockstar Games, Rockstar Studios

The Trailer

My Reaction

Red Dead Redemption 2 is widely regarded as one of the greatest games of all time, and it’s easy to see why. The graphics, the storytelling and the detailed open world all come together into something that feels like a genuine artistic achievement, even if the pacing and some of the gameplay mechanics can be frustrating at times.

item.title: Reaction The Light by Common
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

I was blown away from the show. Seeing Common perform live put this song in a completely different light and it’s stuck with me ever since.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“The Light” was released in 2000 on Common’s album Like Water for Chocolate, produced by James Poyser and built around a sample of Bobby Caldwell’s “Open Your Eyes.” Part of the Soulquarians-era work that defined the album’s sound, it became one of Common’s biggest and most accessible singles; a warm, melodic love song that stood apart from the more conscious, abstract material he was known for at the time.

item.title: Gaming: Team Fortress 2
item.description:
Game/Publisher/Developer

Team Fortress 2, Valve, Valve. Released 10 October, 2007.

The Trailer

My Reaction

Team Fortress 2 works because every class gives you something clear to do, and then throws you into matches where everything is happening at once. You are dodging rockets, chasing objectives, trying to stay alive and relying on teammates at the same time.

item.title: Reaction Wake Me Up When September Ends by Green Day
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

I was in middle school when I heard Green Day for the first time. Evolving to recent days, Green Day was my jam. Father’s Day just passed and I’m missing my father, so this one hit differently this time around.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“Wake Me Up When September Ends” was written by Billie Joe Armstrong and released in 2004 on Green Day’s album American Idiot. Armstrong wrote it about losing his own father to cancer when he was just 10 years old and that grief runs through every line. It became one of the band’s biggest singles and despite later being associated with other tragedies because of its timing, remains at its core a deeply personal song about loss.

item.title: Gaming: Contra
item.description:
Game/Publisher/Developer

Contra, Konami, Konami (NES)

The Trailer

My Reaction

Contra was an instant classic the moment it landed on NES. The run-and-gun action is relentless, the co-op is a blast and the difficulty makes every stage clear feel earned.

Overview

Contra is a run-and-gun action game originally released in arcades by Konami in 1987 before being ported to the Nintendo Entertainment System in 1988. Players control Bill Rizer or Lance Bean, two commandos fighting against the alien organization Red Falcon. Across a series of relentless side-scrolling and overhead stages, players blast through waves of enemies, collect weapon power-ups and face down some of the toughest boss fights of the era. Two-player co-op made it a defining couch experience on NES.

item.title: Reaction Stormy Monday by B.B. King
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

B.B. King is one of the greats, all time. Hearing him take on “Stormy Monday” live is a reminder of exactly why.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“Stormy Monday” started life as “Call It Stormy Monday (But Tuesday Is Just as Bad),” written and recorded by T-Bone Walker in 1947. It became one of the most covered blues standards of all time, and B.B. King made it a regular part of his live shows over the decades, putting his own stamp on the phrasing, the guitar work and the emotion behind it.

item.title: Gaming: Counter-Strike
item.description:
Game/Publisher/Developer

Counter-Strike, Valve / Sierra Studios, Valve (originally a Half-Life mod by Minh Le and Jess Cliffe)

The Trailer

My Reaction

Counter-Strike was very unique in 2000. Multiplayer gaming was still in an early stage and Counter-Strike changed the game completely. We played it in the dorms over T1, and it was unlike anything else; a tactical team-based shooter when most multiplayer shooters were still pure run-and-gun. I was into it.

item.title: Reaction Ain't No Sunshine by Bill Withers
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

This is soul music. They just don’t make these anymore. Bill Withers was a musical genius and “Ain’t No Sunshine” is proof of it in under two and a half minutes.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“Ain’t No Sunshine” was written and performed by Bill Withers, released in 1971 on his debut album Just As I Am. It reached number three on the Billboard Hot 100 and won the Grammy Award for Best Rhythm & Blues Song. Withers famously repeated the line “I know” 26 times in place of a third verse he hadn’t finished writing; his producers convinced him to leave it in, and it became one of the most memorable moments in soul music.

item.title: Gaming: NHL 95
item.description:
Game/Publisher/Developer

NHL 95, EA Sports / THQ (Game Boy), High Score Productions/Double Diamond Sports (Genesis), Visual Concepts (SNES), Realtime Associates Seattle (Game Gear), Probe Entertainment (Game Boy), EA Sports (DOS)

The Trailer

My Reaction

I played this one on PC. A friend got me going on PC games and we played NHL 95 together constantly. It was so fun. One of those games that’s tied directly to a memory of who you played it with, not just what it was.

item.title: Reaction If I Ruled the World by Nas ft. Lauryn Hill
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

Nas has always been one of the sharpest voices in hip-hop and “If I Ruled the World” is one of his most enduring records. Pairing him with Lauryn Hill on the hook turns an already great song into something timeless.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“If I Ruled the World (Imagine That)” was released in 1996 on Nas’s second album, It Was Written. The track samples Kurtis Blow’s 1985 song of the same name and pairs Nas’s verses with a hook sung by Lauryn Hill of The Fugees. It became one of Nas’s biggest commercial hits, reaching the top of the Billboard Hot Rap Songs chart, and remains one of the most recognisable hip-hop singles of the era.

item.title: Gaming: Watch Dogs 2
item.description:
Game/Publisher/Developer

Watch Dogs 2, Ubisoft, Ubisoft Montreal

The Trailer

My Reaction

Watch Dogs 2 is a gorgeous game. The San Francisco setting is beautifully realised and the hacking mechanics make the open world feel genuinely different from anything else out there. The tone is lighter and more fun than the first game and it works. An easy buy.

item.title: Reaction 'Round Midnight by Thelonious Monk
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

Thelonious Monk is one of the great jazz pianists. His approach to the instrument is unlike anyone else; angular, unpredictable and completely his own. “‘Round Midnight” is the song that defines him and it demanded a reaction.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“‘Round Midnight” was written by Thelonious Monk around 1944 and has since become one of the most recorded jazz standards in history. The title captures the mood perfectly; a piece that sounds like the loneliness and stillness of the late-night hours. Monk’s harmonic language was ahead of its time, full of dissonance and surprise, and nowhere is that more apparent than here. Miles Davis, Coltrane and countless others have covered it, but Monk’s own recordings remain the definitive versions.

item.title: Gaming: Warcraft II
item.description:
Game/Publisher/Developer

Warcraft II, NA: Davidson & Associates / EU: Zablac Entertainment, Blizzard Entertainment

My Reaction

Warcraft II hooked me immediately. The choice between humans and orcs felt meaningful and the moment you start building your base and training units the game just pulls you in. The strategic depth was unlike anything else at the time. This was an easy buy and one I kept coming back to.

Overview

Warcraft II: Tides of Darkness is a real-time strategy game developed by Blizzard Entertainment and released in 1995. Players choose between the Alliance (humans) and the Horde (orcs), each with unique units, buildings and abilities. The goal is to gather resources; gold, wood and oil; build a base, train an army and crush the enemy. It features a full campaign for both factions, custom scenarios and multiplayer. A landmark title that helped define the RTS genre.

item.title: Reaction Everlong by Foo Fighters
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

Foo Fighters have been a constant in my life and “Everlong” is the song that defines them. It’s one of those tracks that never loses its grip. The energy, the melody and the emotion. It all comes together perfectly. Hard to ignore a song this good.

About This Song

“Everlong” was released in 1997 on Foo Fighters’ second album The Colour and the Shape. Dave Grohl wrote it during a period of personal upheaval. It shows the song carries a rawness and urgency that’s hard to fake. It became the band’s signature track and is widely regarded as one of the dominant alternative rock songs ever recorded. The music video, directed by Michel Gondry.

item.title: Reaction The Middle by Jimmy Eat World
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

Jimmy Eat World is one of those bands that has been with me for a long time. “The Middle” is one of those songs that hits you at the right moment in life and just sticks. It’s a song about not giving up on yourself and that message never gets old.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“The Middle” was released in 2001 on Jimmy Eat World’s breakout album Bleed American. Frontman Jim Adkins wrote it as an encouragement to younger fans who were struggling to fit in. A reminder that the awkwardness and self-doubt of adolescence is temporary. The song reached the top five on the Billboard Hot 100 and became one of the defining alternative rock tracks of the early 2000s. It still resonates just as strongly today.

item.title: Gaming: 7 Days To Die
item.description:
Game/Publisher/Developer

7 Days To Die, The Fun Pimps/Telltale Publishing, The Fun Pimps

First Look

My first impression wasn’t great. The graphics didn’t do much to grab my attention right away.

Trailer

My Reaction

What won me over was the map size, it’s massive. The game ran very smoothly, movement felt fluid and responsive. The performance held up well throughout. My only real complaint was the graphics; it’s not the prettiest game out there. Even so, I went ahead and bought 7 Days to Die.

item.title: Gaming: Grand Theft Auto 5
item.description:
Game/Publisher/Developer

Grand Theft Auto 5, Rockstar Games, Rockstar North

First Look

The graphics were stunning for 2013. Los Santos felt alive in a way no open-world game had managed before; the scale, the detail and the sheer ambition of it all was immediately apparent.

The Trailer

My Reaction

The trailer sold it immediately. Seeing the three-protagonist structure was a surprise; switching between Michael, Trevor and Franklin mid-mission was something fresh for the series. The world looked massive, the story looked cinematic and the chaos looked as fun as ever. This was an easy buy.

item.title: About
item.description:
A website about the things I cherish, games and music.

Gaming

I’ve been playing games for as long as I can remember. From the pixelated chaos of Doom and the endlessly satisfying loop of Tetris. Furthermore, the grand strategy and multiplayer of Warcraft II and the brutal competitiveness of Counter-Strike.

Some favorites that have stuck with me over the years:

Classics & Strategy Doom · Tetris · NHL 1995 · Master of Orion · Heroes of Might and Magic · Warcraft II · Age of Empires II · Starcraft
